lorehq
─[2]-Primitives──

─[3]-Guides──
CommandsCustomizationGuidesPlatform SupportWorking Across Repos
─[4]-Workflow──
Active Work Architecture
─[ Community and Release Governance ]─ ┐

Community and Release Governance

Goal

Improve external trust and contribution quality by formalizing community policies and introducing lightweight release governance.

Scope

1. Community scaffolding

  • Add LICENSE file (Apache-2.0, full text)
  • Add CONTRIBUTING.md with contribution flow, required checks, and PR expectations
  • Add CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md (Contributor Covenant v2.1)
  • Add GitHub issue templates: bug report and feature request (YAML-form)
  • Add .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/config.yml with “Question” link to docs site
  • Add GitHub pull request template

2. Release discipline

  • Define 0.x release policy and compatibility expectations
  • Add .github/release.yml for auto-generated release note categories
  • Publish first tagged release (v0.1.0) with release notes
  • Add CHANGELOG.md (Keep a Changelog format, initial v0.1.0 entry)
  • Document when to cut patch/minor releases
  • Release cadence: milestone-based 0.x tied to roadmap phases (no calendar pressure)

3. Public maturity signaling

  • Add concise public roadmap summary in README.md — moved to Launch Content and Distribution
  • Add version badge to README.md — moved to Launch Content and Distribution
  • Clarify tool support posture (Claude Code primary, OpenCode supported, Cursor planned)
  • Ensure maturity language is consistent across top-level docs — moved to Launch Content and Distribution

3.5 Public re-open gate

  • Keep repository private during setup/hardening window
  • Define launch-day public re-open checklist (privacy scrub, governance/security links, release notes ready)
  • Record re-open decision with date and owner

4. Maintainer continuity

  • Add minimal maintainer checklist for release and security response
  • Document review/merge expectations and fallback ownership notes

Deliverables

Create:

  • LICENSE — Apache-2.0 full text
  • CONTRIBUTING.md — contribution flow, validation commands, PR expectations
  • CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md — Contributor Covenant v2.1
  • CHANGELOG.md — Keep a Changelog format, initial v0.1.0 entry
  • .github/release.yml — release note categories for auto-generated notes
  • .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/bug_report.yml — YAML-form bug report template
  • .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/feature_request.yml — YAML-form feature request template
  • .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/config.yml — “Question” link to docs site
  • .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md — validation checklist

Contribute requirements to:

  • README.md — community section, public roadmap summary, version badge — fully owned by Launch Content and Distribution

Tag (after security hardening):

  • git tag -a v0.1.0 — first release

Design Decisions

  • Release cadence: milestone-based 0.x (operator decision 2026-02-11)
  • Issue templates: minimal scope — bug report + feature request only (YAML-form)
  • First version: v0.1.0 (not v0.9.0 — honest about POC status)

Success Criteria

  • LICENSE file exists (Apache-2.0 full text)
  • CONTRIBUTING.md, CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md, and all templates are present and linked
  • .github/release.yml configures release note categories
  • .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/config.yml links questions to docs site
  • At least one 0.x tag/release exists with notes
  • CHANGELOG.md exists in Keep a Changelog format
  • README includes community section, public roadmap summary, and version badge — owned by Launch Content and Distribution
  • Maintainer checklist exists for release/security routines
  • Public re-open gate checklist is defined and used before visibility change

Dependencies

  • Complements Security and Compliance Baseline for enterprise intake
  • Informs Adoption Experience plan messaging and examples
  • First release (v0.1.0) gated on security hardening completion

Test Plan

bash scripts/validate-consistency.sh
mkdocs build --strict
PgUp/PgDn: scroll b: bulk commands q: quit x: menu ← → ↑ ↓: navigate
Donate 0.24.4
